title: Ultimatum

jokes.text: We're often compared to the Guardian Angels, but we could not be more different from them. Seriously, we are nothing like the Guardian Angels. I mean, broad strokes purposes, sure. Think Guardian Angels.
jokes.setup: Dwight claiming his group is completely different from Guardian Angels
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself by saying they're basically the same thing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - contradicting himself within seconds while being completely oblivious to it. Classic character-specific absurdity.

jokes.text: One of our many recent good deeds, we set out to capture the Scranton Strangler. Mission accomplished. Not by us.
jokes.setup: Claiming mission accomplished on catching the Strangler
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting they didn't actually do it
jokes.explanation: Perfectly constructed misdirection with economy of language. The casual 'Not by us' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: No, no, no, no, no. I see how this movie ends. You make fun of me, everyone laughs. (SARCASTICALLY) Ha, ha, ha. Screw you.
jokes.setup: Dwight anticipating mockery from Jim's presence
jokes.punchline: Being overly dramatic and defensive about predictable teasing
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Dwight's self-awareness but paranoia. Meta element works for the show's tone.

jokes.text: Knights stayed outside to play flashlight tag.
jokes.setup: Reading official meeting minutes about serious patrol activities
jokes.punchline: Revealing they just played a children's game
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective contrast between serious presentation and childish activity. Economy of words makes it work.

jokes.text: Oh, come on. That sounds awesome. It was pretty cool. Was it? It was fun. It was... It was pretty awesome.
jokes.setup: Jim expressing genuine interest in flashlight tag
jokes.punchline: Dwight getting flustered trying to defend something that actually was fun
jokes.explanation: Nice moment of Dwight being caught between embarrassment and genuine enthusiasm. Shows character depth.

jokes.text: Holly Partridge Flax, otherwise known as Holly, otherwise otherwise known as a living, breathing angel
jokes.setup: Formal introduction format
jokes.punchline: Michael's over-the-top romantic description and awkward 'otherwise otherwise'
jokes.explanation: Pure Michael Scott - awkward phrasing combined with excessive romanticism. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: If she's not engaged, in all honesty, I may just burn this whole place to the ground out of happiness. Either way, I am going to need some talking down.
jokes.setup: Michael describing extreme reactions to both outcomes
jokes.punchline: Burning down the office from happiness, treating both outcomes as equally destructive
jokes.explanation: Excellent Michael logic - equally extreme reactions to opposite outcomes. Great character consistency with his dramatic tendencies.

jokes.text: Michael, I know what you're thinking. Holly is engaged to somebody else and you want to kill yourself. That may seem like a good idea, but it's not. (LAUGHING) Snot. Sorry, it sounded like you said, 'It's snot.' I'm so sorry.
jokes.setup: Michael giving himself serious advice about suicide
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted by mishearing 'it's not' as 'it's snot' in the middle of serious topic
jokes.explanation: Masterful Michael moment - derailing his own serious pep talk with juvenile humor. Perfect character integration and timing.

jokes.text: Wow, did your baby draw that?
jokes.setup: Erin presenting her handmade resolution board
jokes.punchline: Angela's withering assessment of Erin's craft skills
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela - cutting and direct. Economy of words makes the insult land harder.

jokes.text: The glitter is blinding.
jokes.setup: Following Angela's criticism of the craft project
jokes.punchline: Stanley's matter-of-fact complaint about excessive glitter
jokes.explanation: Solid Stanley deadpan. Simple but fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: And you know her husband's in a wheelchair, right?
jokes.setup: Erin praising Sue from Vance Refrigeration
jokes.punchline: Phyllis randomly bringing up irrelevant tragic information
jokes.explanation: Classic Phyllis - bringing up awkward personal information for no reason. Dark humor executed well.

jokes.text: Bears sad, worms happy. Come on, Erin.
jokes.setup: Michael sorting items by emotional outcome
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary assignment of emotions to gummy candy based on animal type
jokes.explanation: Michael's weird logic on display. The impatient 'Come on, Erin' sells his frustration at her not understanding his system.

jokes.text: Sad. Something to break.
jokes.setup: Erin asking if ukuleles are happy
jokes.punchline: Michael planning to destroy musical instruments if he's sad
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and economical. Shows Michael's destructive planning while maintaining the innocent ukulele context.

jokes.text: I hope she doesn't call us up on stage, that would suck. 'Hey, you think you could do my job better?' 'I don't know.' 'What's your name?' 'Michael.'
jokes.setup: Michael worried about Paula Poundstone calling them on stage
jokes.punchline: Acting out the awkward interaction he's imagining
jokes.explanation: Nice bit of Michael's anxiety manifesting as imagined scenarios. Relatable fear executed in character.

jokes.text: And if not, he'll be avoiding the biggest mistake of his life.
jokes.setup: Jim saying he's excited for Michael either way regarding Holly
jokes.punchline: Suggesting that NOT getting Holly would actually be lucky for Michael
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim - supportive on the surface but subtly suggesting Michael might be better off. Good ironic structure.

jokes.text: His name is New Year's Resolution Board. (STAGGERING) Shut up. Hello.
jokes.setup: Erin introducing the board as a person
jokes.punchline: Having a full conversation with an inanimate object, complete with physical interaction
jokes.explanation: Pure Erin weirdness. The staggering and conversation with the board shows her childlike approach to everything.

jokes.text: Put her there. Give me a good firm one. Ooh, that's a good, firm handshake. You're hired. Yes, well let's get right into the Anderson account, shall we? Okay. Yes, yes, right away.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to feel Holly's hand for a ring through handshake
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fake business meeting to justify extended hand contact
jokes.explanation: Great cringe comedy - Michael's desperate attempt to check for ring through elaborate charade. Holly playing along makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey. Want me to crank the thermostat to 90? Smoke her out? She can't keep those mittens on forever.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to figure out if Holly has an engagement ring under mittens
jokes.punchline: Jim suggesting an elaborate heating scheme to force mitten removal
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and specific plan. 'Smoke her out' is perfect phrasing, treating mittens like a military operation.

jokes.text: Hey, right back at you, bitch.
jokes.setup: Holly saying 'Hey' in what seems like a greeting
jokes.punchline: Michael interpreting it as an insult and responding aggressively
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character moment. Michael's misinterpretation and aggressive response is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: What? Shut that off. I'm not listening to the music.
jokes.setup: Michael telling Erin to breathe and calm down
jokes.punchline: Erin secretly listening to upbeat music while pretending to follow instructions
jokes.explanation: Solid Erin moment - her innocent cheating on Michael's calming exercise. Shows her childlike approach to rules.

jokes.text: Michael dance-off!
jokes.setup: Michael being extremely happy about Holly not being engaged
jokes.punchline: Challenging someone to a dance battle in the office
jokes.explanation: Pure Michael joy expressed inappropriately. Physical comedy enhanced by his complete lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: I've achieved plenty, and there's no better than the best.
jokes.setup: Erin asking Dwight about his resolution
jokes.punchline: Dwight claiming he's already perfect and needs no improvement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight arrogance. The phrasing 'no better than the best' is awkwardly confident in typical Dwight fashion.

jokes.text: Learn to cook for one.
jokes.setup: Kevin sharing his resolution
jokes.punchline: Sadly accepting his permanent single status
jokes.explanation: Economical and character-specific sadness. The resignation in the phrase captures Kevin's romantic situation perfectly.

jokes.text: No, some people just don't meet someone. I'm fine with it. Really. This is not a pity party. It's not a party at all. It's just sad.
jokes.setup: Pam suggesting Kevin might meet someone
jokes.punchline: Kevin's increasingly depressing self-assessment, ending with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure. 'It's not a party at all. It's just sad.' is perfectly crafted - subverts the idiom beautifully.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I was confused 'cause you accidentally wrote 'The senator.'
jokes.setup: Angela mentioning her boyfriend 'the senator'
jokes.punchline: Oscar's passive-aggressive correction implying pretentiousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar - pedantic correction disguised as helpfulness. The 'accidentally' is beautifully condescending.

jokes.text: My resolution is 'Meet a loose woman.'
jokes.setup: Kevin deciding to change his resolution after hearing Ryan's
jokes.punchline: Crude and inappropriate resolution delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin crudeness but relatively predictable. The matter-of-fact delivery saves it from being completely hacky.

jokes.text: 'Hey, what book is that?' 'Cool, let's hang out tonight.' 'Sex already? Whoa.'
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining how to pick up women at bookstores
jokes.punchline: Completely unrealistic progression from book question to immediate sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan delusion - completely unrealistic pickup scenario delivered with total confidence. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: (IMITATING E.T.) E.T. phone Holly. Holly like phone? (IMITATING E.T.) Holly misses old phone.
jokes.setup: Michael seeing Holly with a new phone
jokes.punchline: Doing an extended E.T. impression about phones
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael reference humor - relies on impression rather than wit. Gets mild laughs from commitment but not clever.

jokes.text: Please stop.
jokes.setup: Michael's extended E.T. impression
jokes.punchline: Kelly's exasperated plea for it to end
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction. Economy and relatability make it work as audience surrogate.

jokes.text: Do you think she's already dating a different A.J.?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to understand Holly's relationship status
jokes.punchline: Suggesting she might be dating another person with the same initials
jokes.explanation: Great Michael logic leap. The specificity of 'different A.J.' shows his mind working in wonderfully illogical ways.

jokes.text: When you broke it off with Roy did you still tell him you loved him? No. But you did still love him. I'm not gonna have... Do you love him now? No!
jokes.setup: Michael asking Pam about her relationship with Roy for advice
jokes.punchline: Awkward rapid-fire personal questions culminating in current feelings
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment showing Michael's inappropriate personal questions. Pam's increasing discomfort drives the comedy.

jokes.text: 'Oh, hi, friend.' (EXCLAIMS IN DISGUST)
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he won't talk to Holly directly
jokes.punchline: Acting out his fear of being friend-zoned with physical revulsion
jokes.explanation: Good Michael moment - his horror at being friendzoned expressed through acting. Physical reaction sells the joke.

jokes.text: They could put us out of business, you know? Heard those machines hold, like, 10 books at once. Actually, it's 10,000. Holy (BLEEP). What?
jokes.setup: Kevin worried about e-readers affecting paper company
jokes.punchline: Being completely shocked when corrected about capacity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from 10 to 10,000. Kevin's shock and profanity feel genuine and in character.

jokes.text: So light. Like a croissant.
jokes.setup: Kevin examining the e-reader
jokes.punchline: Comparing advanced technology to a pastry
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kevin comparison - completely unexpected but perfectly in character. 'Like a croissant' is beautifully specific and absurd.

jokes.text: Why did we pretend like we work here? Is that what we were doing?
jokes.setup: Andy and Darryl at the bookstore
jokes.punchline: Confusion about whether they were pretending to be employees
jokes.explanation: Mild confusion comedy. Works as a small moment but nothing exceptional.

jokes.text: A book about oceans. Oh, really? What else? Let me see. That's porn. Pornography. Old lady. Nasty porn.
jokes.setup: Andy asking what Darryl bought
jokes.punchline: Revealing increasingly specific and disturbing porn details
jokes.explanation: Low-hanging fruit comedy. Gets reaction through shock value rather than wit.

jokes.text: I went to Portugal. I went to Portugal. Oh, wow. You went together? No. No.
jokes.setup: Pam and Erin both claiming to have been to Portugal for break
jokes.punchline: Awkward realization they're both lying about the same fake trip
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. The awkwardness of being caught in the same lie drives the humor effectively.

jokes.text: I mean, not even for a week to make him buy you a present?
jokes.setup: Holly explaining she didn't break up with A.J.
jokes.punchline: Erin's naive suggestion about relationship manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good Erin naivety - treating relationship manipulation as normal strategy. Shows her limited romantic experience.

jokes.text: The day shift at a strip club? You can't un-see that.
jokes.setup: Dwight wanting to go to strip club at noon on Monday
jokes.punchline: Darryl's warning about the horrors of daytime strippers
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational comedy. 'You can't un-see that' is perfectly ominous and economical.

jokes.text: Chicks fall down, need help getting back up. Roller derby practice. (GROWLING JOYFULLY)
jokes.setup: Darryl explaining opportunities at roller rink
jokes.punchline: Getting increasingly excited about aggressive roller derby women
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from helpful to predatory excitement. The growl sells Darryl's attraction to tough women.

jokes.text: Well, my goal was to learn a new word every single day. And I must say that it is going immensely.
jokes.setup: Erin describing her vocabulary resolution
jokes.punchline: Misusing 'immensely' while trying to show off new vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin - trying to sound smart while demonstrating the opposite. Word misuse comedy executed well.

jokes.text: Intercepted. Hey... Gabe. Oops-e-doops.
jokes.setup: Gabe giving Erin a reward
jokes.punchline: Michael intercepting the gift and awkward recovery when caught
jokes.explanation: Solid Michael moment - childish behavior followed by cutesy recovery. 'Oops-e-doops' is perfectly infantile.

jokes.text: I want to do a cartwheel. But real casual, like. Not make a big deal out of it. But I know everybody saw it, just one stunning, gorgeous cartwheel.
jokes.setup: Creed explaining his resolution
jokes.punchline: Wanting to be simultaneously casual and spectacular about a cartwheel
jokes.explanation: Fantastic Creed moment - the contradiction between casual and stunning is perfectly absurd. Sets up running gag beautifully.

jokes.text: 12:01, January 1st. Bam! Blood everywhere.
jokes.setup: Michael saying he completed his flossing resolution
jokes.punchline: Immediate violent consequences of first attempt at flossing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and timing. The immediate violence of neglected dental hygiene is relatable and well-executed.

jokes.text: And you know what's gonna be on your tombstone? 'Loser.'
jokes.setup: Michael berating Creed for not doing cartwheels
jokes.punchline: Threatening him with a harsh tombstone inscription
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to death threat over cartwheels. Shows Michael's inappropriate intensity about resolutions.

jokes.text: My tombstone's already made, thank you.
jokes.setup: Michael threatening Creed's tombstone
jokes.punchline: Creed casually revealing he's already prepared for death
jokes.explanation: Excellent Creed moment - matter-of-fact about morbid preparation. Perfect character consistency and unexpected response.

jokes.text: This is a new food for me. How else should I eat it?
jokes.setup: Kevin eating broccoli stem-first
jokes.punchline: Treating vegetables like completely foreign objects
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin moment showing his complete unfamiliarity with healthy food. Believable character ignorance.

jokes.text: Can I get some Cheez Whiz? Or Hollandaise?
jokes.setup: Kevin struggling to eat plain broccoli
jokes.punchline: Requesting completely inappropriate condiments for vegetables
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin - trying to make vegetables palatable with junk. Hollandaise is an especially absurd choice.

jokes.text: Come on, here comes the airplane. There you go. Open, open, into the hangar, there you go.
jokes.setup: Michael forcing Kevin to eat broccoli
jokes.punchline: Using baby talk and airplane game on adult coworker
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - inappropriate baby talk. Shows Michael's complete lack of boundaries with employees.

jokes.text: You need to get your own life.
jokes.setup: Michael apologizing for his intensity about others' resolutions
jokes.punchline: Holly's blunt assessment of Michael's problem
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest and economical. Holly cuts right to Michael's core issue with perfect directness.

jokes.text: I don't think I'm there yet, boss.
jokes.setup: Michael hoping they can still be friends after traumatizing Kevin
jokes.punchline: Kevin's honest assessment that he's not ready to forgive
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin honesty moment. The 'boss' adds formality that makes it funnier.

jokes.text: How's your fart project coming?
jokes.setup: Ryan discussing his creative projects
jokes.punchline: Kevin's crude reduction of art to bathroom humor
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin crudeness but predictable. The economy saves it from being completely hacky.

jokes.text: I could be pregnant.
jokes.setup: Meredith drinking while others abandon resolutions
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning possible pregnancy while drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith - casual about inappropriate behavior. The timing and economy make it effective dark humor.

jokes.text: Oh, my resolution was to get more attention. Nope, she's lying.
jokes.setup: Meredith claiming her resolution was attention-seeking
jokes.punchline: Ryan immediately calling out the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment - Meredith's obvious attention grab immediately exposed. Ryan's directness makes it work.

jokes.text: I did it. I did a cartwheel. (BLEEP) you! (BLEEP) you!
jokes.setup: Creed finally attempting his cartwheel resolution
jokes.punchline: Claiming success while clearly having failed, then swearing aggressively
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff. Creed's delusional claim of success followed by aggressive swearing is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: His name is Andy. And he roller skates like a Greek God. And you know what? I kind of like hanging out with him.
jokes.setup: Asking if Darryl met someone at the roller rink
jokes.punchline: Darryl describing a romantic interest who turns out to be a man
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character development. Darryl's openness to male friendship subverts expectations nicely.

jokes.text: My resolution? I never want to make Holly cry again. Unless it's from laughing too hard. Or making love too beautifully.
jokes.setup: Michael stating his new resolution about Holly
jokes.punchline: Adding increasingly inappropriate exceptions to his noble goal
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure. Michael can't help but sexualize his sweet sentiment, perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You know how you say something over and over and the words start to sound weird? Going to be just fine. Just fine, just fine, just fine.
jokes.setup: Holly reflecting on her earlier statement about being fine
jokes.punchline: Demonstrating semantic satiation by repeating the phrase until it loses meaning
jokes.explanation: Smart observational comedy about semantic satiation. Shows Holly's intelligence and self-awareness.

jokes.text: I did it! You did? A perfect cartwheel. (LAUGHING) Okay, good! What a rush! That's all I had to do all year.
jokes.setup: Creed finally doing his cartwheel with Darryl's help
jokes.punchline: Creed's complete satisfaction with accomplishing one simple task for the entire year
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback resolution. Creed's satisfaction with minimal achievement is beautifully absurd and character-appropriate.

tmdb_overview: Michael anticipates news about Holly's relationship status; Pam introduces office resolution board.
